title: LAPTOP-0005 (Pahal-ThinkPad-L15): Battery Calibration Needed
summary: This mobile device's battery reporting has become inaccurate, causing it to shut down unexpectedly even when the screen shows remaining charge.

dataSources.label: Battery calibration readings from the past 30 days
Power management logs across 1,815 mobile devices
Manufacturer calibration guidelines

knownLimitations: Battery calibration works best when the user can leave the device undisturbed for several hours. Active travel schedules may delay the process.

reasoningSteps.label: What we found
reasoningSteps.detail: We noticed that the reported battery level changes suddenly and does not match the actual charge remaining.

reasoningSteps.label: Why it matters
reasoningSteps.detail: An uncalibrated battery can cause sudden shutdowns and reduced productivity for users who rely on the device away from their desk.

reasoningSteps.label: What we recommend
reasoningSteps.detail: We recommend performing a full charge and discharge cycle to recalibrate the battery gauge, which should restore accurate reporting.

options.name: Option A
options.title: Option A: Start calibration today
options.description: Send instructions to the user to fully drain and recharge the battery today. No replacement parts are needed.

options.name: Option B
options.title: Option B: Replace battery instead
options.description: Arrange for a battery replacement if the device is still under warranty or if calibration has failed before.

blastRadius.description: Executing this workflow will cause temporary network isolation. Low risk to dependent services.

title: LAPTOP-0010 (Thomas-MacBook-Pro-14): Critical Patch Missing
summary: This device is missing 4 security patches released in the last 30 days. Two of them fix vulnerabilities that attackers are actively using right now.

dataSources.label: Windows Update patch history for this device (last 90 days)
Microsoft Security Bulletin database — June 2024
Patch compliance records across 1,903 similar Ubuntu 22.04 LTS devices in your fleet

knownLimitations: This recommendation assumes the device has a stable internet connection for patch download. Devices on metered or VPN-only connections may need a different deployment method.

reasoningSteps.label: What we found
reasoningSteps.detail: We checked this device's update history and found 4 patches missing that were released between May 15 and June 10, 2024.

reasoningSteps.label: Why it matters
reasoningSteps.detail: Two of these patches fix security gaps that real attackers are currently exploiting. Devices missing these patches are far more likely to be compromised based on your fleet's own incident history over the past year.

reasoningSteps.label: What we recommend
reasoningSteps.detail: Applying all 4 patches now brings this device back to full compliance and removes the known risk. The update takes approximately 15 minutes and requires one restart.

options.name: Option A
options.title: Option A: Apply all patches now
options.description: Push all 4 missing patches immediately during the next maintenance window. Device will restart once. Estimated downtime: 15 minutes.

options.name: Option B
options.title: Option B: Schedule for tonight
options.description: Queue the patch deployment for 11 PM tonight when the device is less likely to be in active use. Risk remains elevated until then.

blastRadius.description: Executing this workflow will cause temporary network isolation. Medium risk to dependent services.

title: LAPTOP-0009 (Rushil-Latitude-5430): Firmware Update Available
summary: The firmware that runs this device's hardware is out of date and the manufacturer has released a newer version that fixes known stability and security issues.

dataSources.label: BIOS/UEFI version reported by this device
Manufacturer firmware release notes for model Latitude-5430
Firmware version comparison across 1,903 similar devices

knownLimitations: Firmware updates require the device to stay powered and connected throughout the process. Laptops must be plugged into power to avoid interruption.

reasoningSteps.label: What we found
reasoningSteps.detail: This device is running firmware version 1.08, while the manufacturer's latest tested release is 1.15.

reasoningSteps.label: Why it matters
reasoningSteps.detail: The newer firmware fixes problems that can cause unexpected crashes and weakens protections that keep the device secure at startup.

reasoningSteps.label: What we recommend
reasoningSteps.detail: We recommend scheduling the firmware update during the next maintenance window because it closes those gaps and improves hardware reliability.

options.name: Option A
options.title: Option A: Update firmware now
options.description: Apply the firmware update immediately. The device will reboot once and should be ready within a few minutes.

options.name: Option B
options.title: Option B: Schedule for next maintenance window
options.description: Queue the firmware update for the next planned maintenance window. The device remains on the older firmware until then.

blastRadius.description: Executing this workflow will cause temporary network isolation. High risk to dependent services.

title: MOBILE-0010 (Nathaniel-iPhone-14): Storage Health Warning
summary: This device is running low on free disk space, which can slow down work and prevent important updates from installing correctly.

dataSources.label: Disk usage telemetry for this device over the past 60 days
Fleet maintenance records covering 1,742 similar Mobile devices
Support ticket history from the last 12 months

knownLimitations: The cleanup estimate assumes typical user files and cached data. Devices with very large project files may need manual review before automatic cleanup.

reasoningSteps.label: What we found
reasoningSteps.detail: We found that this device's main drive is more than 85% full, leaving little room for updates or temporary files.

reasoningSteps.label: Why it matters
reasoningSteps.detail: When disk space drops this low, systems become slow and security updates can fail. Similar devices in your fleet with the same issue generated twice as many support tickets.

reasoningSteps.label: What we recommend
reasoningSteps.detail: We recommend running an automated cleanup to remove old temporary files and cached data, which should free enough space and restore normal performance.

options.name: Option A
options.title: Option A: Run cleanup now
options.description: Start the automated cleanup tool immediately to remove temporary and cached files. No restart is required.

options.name: Option B
options.title: Option B: Ask user to free space
options.description: Send a polite request to the assigned user to move personal files to cloud storage before the cleanup runs.

blastRadius.description: Executing this workflow will cause temporary network isolation. Critical risk to dependent services.
